questions.subject: Social Studies

questions.explanation: Lusaka City was named after the headman Mwalusaka.

questions.explanation: Malnutrition is most directly caused by a lack of enough food to meet nutritional needs.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Goods can be bought and sold faster around the world by using ...
questions.explanation: The internet allows goods to be bought and sold faster around the world through e-commerce and online transactions.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: In the Iron Age period, the role of men was ...
questions.explanation: During the Iron Age, hunting was typically a role performed by men, while cooking, pottery, and weaving were often associated with women.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The sending and receiving of information is known as ...
questions.explanation: The sending and receiving of information is the definition of communication.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: People living in warring countries are less productive because ...
questions.explanation: War creates instability, which disrupts normal economic and social activities, reducing productivity.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Zambia can improve her economy through ...
questions.explanation: Agriculture is a productive economic activity through which Zambia can improve its economy, unlike the other options listed.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Road signs are erected to ...

options.A: encourage road users to be careless.
options.D: supply all road users with information.

questions.explanation: Road signs are erected to supply all road users, not just one group, with important information for safety.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: A family provides ... to its members
questions.explanation: A family provides security to its members, offering protection and emotional support.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: On which of the following rivers is the Victoria Falls found?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Falls is found on the Zambezi River.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The TAZARA Railway line links the port of Dar-es-salaam in Tanzania to ... in Zambia.
questions.explanation: The TAZARA Railway line links the port of Dar-es-Salaam in Tanzania to Kapiri Mposhi in Zambia.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Working well with other people in the community shows a citizen's ...
questions.explanation: Working well with others in the community demonstrates a citizen's sense of responsibility towards their society.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Islamic month of fasting is called ...
questions.explanation: Ramadan is the Islamic month of fasting observed by Muslims worldwide.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: A ... can be used in any place where there is a signal.
questions.explanation: A mobile phone can be used anywhere there is network signal, unlike a fixed telephone line or a letter.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... refers to the movement of people from rural to urban areas.
questions.explanation: Rural-urban migration is the specific term for the movement of people from rural to urban areas.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The period when boys and girls become sexually active is called ...
questions.explanation: Puberty is the period during which boys and girls become sexually active and undergo physical maturation.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: In which province is Ing'ombe illede found?
questions.explanation: Ing'ombe Ilede, an important archaeological site, is found in Southern Province (in Siavonga).

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Forgiveness is one way of overcoming ...
questions.explanation: Forgiveness helps a person overcome feelings of revenge towards someone who has wronged them.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Bad behaviour can be corrected through ...
questions.explanation: Discipline is the recognised method of correcting bad behaviour.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The zebra on the coat of arms represents Zambia's ...
questions.explanation: The zebra on Zambia's Coat of Arms represents the country's wildlife.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which one of the following is a cultural celebration?
questions.explanation: An Initiation Ceremony is a cultural celebration, while Diwali, Good Friday, and Ramadan are religious observances.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: What is the meaning of the road sign below?
questions.explanation: The triangular sign showing two converging vertical lines represents a narrowing road ahead, warning drivers that the road width decreases.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: A good leader has the following qualities except ...
questions.explanation: Rudeness is a negative trait and is the exception, as honesty, integrity, and openness are all positive leadership qualities.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Wealth generation is when business ...

options.A: makes less money than put in.
options.C: makes more than it puts in.

questions.explanation: Wealth generation occurs when a business makes more money than what was originally invested.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... is giving out items of value to someone in order to receive a favour.
questions.explanation: Giving out items of value to receive a favour in return is the definition of corruption, specifically bribery.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Using an underage person as a solider is an example of ... rights violation.
questions.explanation: Recruiting an underage person as a soldier is a violation of child rights.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: To reduce pollution, we need to ...

options.B: pour oil and acid in water.

questions.explanation: Managing waste effectively is the positive practice that helps reduce pollution, unlike the other harmful options.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which of the following types of climates has rainfall throughout the year?
questions.explanation: The Equatorial climate is characterised by rainfall throughout the year, unlike Mediterranean, Monsoon, and Savanna climates which have distinct seasons.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Deforestation causes ...
questions.explanation: Deforestation removes trees that help retain moisture and promote rainfall, leading to drought conditions.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: What natural vegetation makes up most of the steppes?
questions.explanation: Steppes are characterised mostly by grassland vegetation.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Apart from using cash money, a ... can be used to buy goods and services.
questions.explanation: A debit card can be used to buy goods and services as an alternative to cash money.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which one of the following organisation resolves conflicts and prevents wars?

options.C: United Nations International Children's Emergence Fund

questions.explanation: The United Nations is the organisation specifically tasked with resolving conflicts and preventing wars between nations.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: World population growth is not caused by ...
questions.explanation: Poor medical care would increase death rates rather than contribute to population growth, making it the exception among the options.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Informal markets start up because of ...
questions.explanation: Informal markets often start up because of a lack of formal work opportunities, prompting people to create their own income sources.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... is the action of buying and selling goods.
questions.explanation: Trade is defined as the action of buying and selling goods.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... is punishment involving physical harming of a child.
questions.explanation: Corporal punishment specifically refers to punishment involving physical harm to a child.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which one of the following is not the importance of financial stability?
questions.explanation: Expenditure (spending) is not an importance/benefit of financial stability, unlike employment, income, and savings which all contribute positively to financial stability.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which one of the following is the second book of the New Testament?
questions.explanation: Mark is the second book of the New Testament, following Matthew.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is an indicator of free and fair elections?
questions.explanation: A secret ballot is an indicator of free and fair elections, as it protects voters' privacy and choice.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which one of the following environmental factors has influenced agricultural systems?
questions.explanation: Climate is an environmental factor that has significantly influenced agricultural systems, determining which crops can be grown and farming methods used.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... waste breaks down in the environment naturally.
questions.explanation: Bio-degradable waste is waste that breaks down naturally in the environment.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which of the following statements is important for a cyclist to know?

options.A: Do not use hands to indicate to motorists.
options.B: Only ride on the right side of the road.
options.D: Wear bright colours especially at night.

questions.explanation: Wearing bright colours, especially at night, helps a cyclist be easily seen by motorists, promoting road safety.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Kulamba Kubwalo Ceremony is performed by the ... People.
questions.explanation: The Kulamba Kubwalo Ceremony is performed by the Chewa people.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is a duty of every citizen?
questions.explanation: Paying taxes is a recognised duty of every citizen, while the other options are either rights or negative behaviours.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The Moto Moto Museum is found in ... District.
questions.explanation: The Moto Moto Museum is found in Mbala District.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Study the following map of Luapula Province below and answer questions 56 to 60. The main economic activity done by the people who live in the area marked L is ...
questions.explanation: Area L is located near a swamp/lake area on the map, where the main economic activity of residents is fishing.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: ... is the main traditional ceremony of the Lunda People of Luapula Province
questions.explanation: Umutomboko is the main traditional ceremony of the Lunda people of Luapula Province.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The plantation found in the area marked P is for ...
questions.explanation: Area P on the map of Luapula Province, marked as a plantation area, is known for coffee growing.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: The lake marked K is ...
questions.explanation: Lake K, shown on the map of Luapula Province, corresponds to Lake Bangweulu, which is labelled directly on the map.

questions.subject: Social Studies
questions.question: Name the river marked M.
questions.explanation: River M, shown running through Luapula Province on the map, corresponds to the Luapula River, after which the province is named.
